mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate reduces recession risk premiums, leading to immediate multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns show no statistical difference between quarters with above-trend GDPC1 growth and those with below-trend growth (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE declines by >0.2pp in a month are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of falling federal funds rates and stabilizing core inflation compresses real discount rates, increasing the present value of future corporate earnings and lifting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no predictive power for 60-day forward SPX returns in a multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Monthly increases in INDPRO consistently precede negative or zero 5-day SPX returns over a 3-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects broad-based economic resilience, reducing default risk and supporting higher equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward SPX returns are uncorrelated with the direction of monthly PAYEMS changes across all business cycles (correlation coefficient < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: New residential housing construction exceeding a monthly threshold signals recovering consumer confidence and financial system stability, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Months where HOUST exceeds 1000 units are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous expansion of retail sales and contraction in the federal funds rate indicates strong consumer spending supported by cheap credit, which drives immediate equity risk-on behavior over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The cross-product of RSAFS and FEDFUNDS changes fails to predict 5-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a rolling window test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant or falling industrial output compress corporate profit margins and force aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Periods characterized by rising CPIAUCSL and falling INDPRO do not produce significantly lower 60-day forward SPX returns compared to baseline periods (t-test p > 0.05).
